Title: DiagConfig: Configuration Diagnosis of Performance Violations in Configurable Software Systems
Scope Fit Reason1: The paper studies post-deployment diagnosis of performance violations in running configurable software systems. It combines runtime profiling with static taint tracking to trace performance anomalies back to concrete configuration options and affected call chains in deployed Java applications such as Cassandra and H2, which matches our scope of troubleshooting operational misconfigurations.
authors: Zhiming Chen; Pengfei Chen; Peipei Wang; Guangba Yu; Zilong He; Genting Mai

Title: White-box performance-influence models: A profiling and learning approach
Scope Fit Reason1: The paper investigates how configuration options influence performance at the method level in highly configurable software systems. By profiling executions and learning white-box performance-influence models, it helps localize configuration-sensitive code regions and diagnose performance bottlenecks caused by configuration choices, which is relevant to non-crash misconfiguration troubleshooting in our review scope.
Venue_name: 2021 IEEE/ACM 43rd International Conference on Software Engineering
authors: Max Weber; Sven Apel; Norbert Siegmund

Title: Localizing configurations in highly-configurable systems
Scope Fit Reason1: The paper focuses on configuration localization, i.e., mapping source-code entities back to the configuration options that control them in highly configurable systems. This is primarily a program-comprehension and variability-analysis problem that helps developers understand where options take effect, rather than a technique for troubleshooting runtime misconfigurations in deployed systems or diagnosing incorrect user-provided configuration settings after deployment.
Venue_name: 22nd International Systems and Software Product Line Conference (SPLC)
authors: Paul Gazzillo; Ugur Koc; ThanhVu Nguyen; Shiyi Wei
